entry.title: Washington Post: 29 Jun, 2022

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 29 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 29 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-29 08:30AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • China’s first lady Peng Liyuan expected to join Xi Jinping on two-day visit to Hong Kong this week for July 1 celebrations
  • Hong Kong could issue No 1 typhoon warning signal on Wednesday night, forecaster says, ahead of expected visit by Chinese President Xi Jinping
  • Hong Kong’s resilient SMEs have weathered every storm since handover, but can they bounce back from Covid-19?
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong confirms more than 2,000 cases for first time since April as business leader calls for more people to qualify for quarantine-free ‘Return2HK’ scheme
  • Six arrested by Hong Kong police after two cars alleged to be involved in illegal racing crash
  • ‘Piloting ship to weather storm’: Chinese state-run paper highlights ‘vitality’ of Hong Kong’s ‘one country, two systems’ principle ahead of Xi Jinping visit
  • Hong Kong’s economy of the future: plugged into Greater Bay Area, but still a global financial centre?
  • Is Chinese University of Hong Kong head Rocky Tuan a patriot? Vice-chancellor addresses criticism, vows CUHK will link China and West
  • More than 10 journalists denied permission to cover Hong Kong handover anniversary events due to security reasons, reporters’ group says
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong may cut hotel quarantine to 5 days, with 2 more in home isolation, incoming health minister says
  • Maritime authorities yet to announce Jumbo Floating Restaurant’s fate as Hong Kong expert says sinking it might not be possible
  • 3,000 guests, staff to begin hotel quarantine in Hong Kong ahead of Chinese President Xi Jinping’s visit and July 1 celebrations
  • Hong Kong Palace Museum ‘ready to be fully opened’ on July 2, with security checks in place
  • Taiwanese drinks shop owners jailed for up to 7 months over social media posts calling on others to flout Hong Kong’s Covid-19 curbs
  • China tells UN expansion of Nato, or a Nato-like body, into the Asia-Pacific will stir up conflict
  • China’s easing of zero-Covid restraints set to release stocks into bull-market territory for first time in three years
  • China to start building giant telescope to monitor solar winds that can knock out satellites and power grids
  • G7 infrastructure plan to rival Belt and Road Initiative could force Chinese firms to ‘match global standards’
  • Shenzhen tightens security ahead of Chinese president’s trip to Hong Kong
  • Coronavirus: faced with mounting costs, some ask whether China can afford to maintain mass testing
  • US ‘smart city’ tech highlights contrasts with China over privacy and control
  • China-Australia relations: chance for ‘thaw’, but can Beijing, Canberra mend fractured trade ties?
  • US seeks China pressure on Russia to end Ukraine war as it weighs economic options against Beijing
  • Global stagflation risks offer China chance to ‘seize opportunity’ for economic, trade windfall
  • G7 leaders denounce China over Russia relations, human rights and Taiwan friction in statement following summit
  • Quarter of world’s population at risk from once-in-a-century floods
  • Chinese breakthrough lets human brains beam radio waves
  • Chinese video gaming billionaire plans relocation with family abroad after draconian Shanghai lockdown
  • Apple supplier Foxconn starts new hiring spree at world’s largest iPhone factory in China ahead of next product launch
  • China, Nato and how the Ukraine war is spilling over into the Asia-Pacific
  • Australians view China as more a security threat than economic partner, Lowy Institute poll shows
  • Beijing would be wise to learn from the outcry over misuse of Covid-19 health codes
  • China’s vice-president to attend Marcos inauguration in Philippines
  • Taiwan slams Beijing for blocking UN Ocean Conference delegates
  • Africa would welcome G7’s US$600 billion infrastructure push – if it happens
  • China pledges quake aid for Afghanistan but also wants Taliban to take action on terrorism
  • Which tech firms qualify as ‘little giants’? Beijing releases criteria for the ideal industrial enterprise
  • South Korean workers resort to shop-bought snacks as ‘lunch-flation’ starts to bite
  • Hindu man murdered in India over support for Prophet Mohammed remarks
  • What is Indonesia’s Joko Widodo hoping to achieve when he meets Russian President Vladimir Putin in Moscow?
  • Philippine news site Rappler ordered to shut down ahead of Duterte’s exit – but vows to carry on
  • Majority of Australians support defending Taiwan against invasion, survey finds
  • Coronavirus: Indonesia urged to tighten curbs during 4th wave as cases of Omicron subvariants rise
  • Singapore’s US$40 billion mega-port aims to solve shipping chaos
  • Indian women with more than 100 live animals in their luggage arrested at Thai airport
  • Australia says committed to Southeast Asia’s stability, allays fears over Aukus nuclear submarines
  • Philippines’ Marcos Jnr will take presidential oath at venue linked to father’s brutal legacy
  • Russia called a ‘terrorist state’, dozens still missing after Ukraine shopping centre attack
  • Pro-China agents posed as activists to protest US, Canada mines: security firm
  • Russia’s Putin visits ‘friendly’ Central Asia on first trip abroad during war
  • Scotland takes fight for independence vote to UK’s top court
  • At least 52 die in Colombia prison riot fire after mattresses set alight
  • Instagram hides some posts mentioning abortion with ‘sensitive content’ label
  • Finland, Sweden closer to joining Nato after Türkiye deal
  • Ronald Reagan shooter John Hinckley Jnr says sorry for wounding ex-president
  • Angry Trump lunged at Secret Service agent, tried to grab steering wheel to go to US Capitol, aide testifies
  • Amid heatwaves and floods, time is running out for rich nations to fulfil climate pledges and avert catastrophe
  • Ghislaine Maxwell sentenced to 20 years for ‘horrific’ sex crimes
  • 2 arrested after armed jewel heist at art fair in Netherlands
  • Supermarket boss says Britons buying more cheap frozen food as cost of living crisis worsens
  • Chinese red-chip stocks make their presence felt in Hong Kong
  • Foresight and fortune: Hong Kong stock exchange thrives on 25 years of China-driven success as CEO Aguzin writes the next chapter
  • Why China has more to lose from decoupling than the US
  • Electric cars: Nio’s shares lose US$6.1 billion value in Hong Kong, Singapore rout as short-seller Grizzly Research claims carmaker ‘juices revenue’
  • Tesla lays off about 200 Autopilot workers after signalling cuts in a tough economic environment
  • Hong Kong stocks fall as recession worries overtake optimism due to easing quarantine rules while EV makers, led by Nio, drop
  • Climate change, ozone pollution to reduce global crop yields by a fifth, with South Asia most at risk, study shows
  • Sun Hung Kai Properties, Sino Land invest in malls, luring shoppers with upgrades for pet lovers, kids, and NFT fans
  • Alibaba CEO extols positive role e-commerce giant’s technology can play in Chinese society in article for official magazine
  • China’s new antitrust rules could increase Big Tech scrutiny, adding hurdles to start-up acquisitions
  • Misguided geopolitics is derailing decades of globalisation, pushing the most vulnerable to brink of disaster
  • China and Hong Kong mark 25th anniversary of handover with launch of ETF Connect on July 4, ‘a gift’ that will bring their capital markets even closer
  • Hong Kong’s luxury home rents fall as expats leave in droves due to Covid curbs, while New York, Toronto and Singapore see gains
  • Shell, ExxonMobil join study to build one of the world’s largest carbon capture and storage projects in China
  • Shanghai reopening: Disneyland theme park to reopen on June 30, ending its 101-day hiatus as life in China’s commercial hub ambles back to normal
  • Exercise and CBT relieve chronic fatigue from arthritis and other inflammatory diseases, study shows
  • Hong Kong’s Jumbo Floating Restaurant lives on in virtual form, never to sink nor capsize
  • Mono­chromatic minimalism, statement furniture in duplex Hong Kong family home that’s filled with an architect’s little magic tricks
  • Tomato soup noodles, ramen, omakase: Hong Kong restaurant recommendations from a drinks marketer
  • What Hong Kong’s luxury scene was like in the late 90s from those who lived it, and how it has evolved over the last 25 years
  • Racism and hate have no place in K-pop fandoms, but are rife. What can we do?
  • Prior Convictions movie review: Japanese manga adaptation sees Kasumi Arimura reprise her role from TV series as a volunteer probation officer
  • Liquid rhinoplasty: pros and cons of the scalpel-free nose job that is trending in 2022
  • Hong Kong dance performance draws on sense of helplessness over politics and the pandemic, and families’ tough decision to stay or go
  • Dior’s seaside garden at Paris Fashion Week: Kim Jones’ spring/summer 2023 menswear collection for the maison married suits with streetwear – and destructured gardening clothes?
  • Is Asia’s richest man also India’s most generous? Gautam Adani celebrated his 60th birthday with a US$7.7 billion donation, skipping an A-list Bollywood bash for a low-key family party
  • Paris Fashion Week: Celine channelled David Bowie androgyny in its spring/summer 2023 menswear show by Hedi Slimane, with rock star sunglasses, sequins and suits
  • 10 of the longest-lasting LGBT celebrity couples: from Michael Kors’ intern love affair and Elton John’s four-decade relationship, to RuPaul’s long-distance marriage and George Takei’s 18-year secret
  • Inside 4 tech billionaires’ new Malibu mansions: Google’s Sergey Brin, WhatsApp’s Jan Koum, Apple’s Laurene Powell Jobs and venture capitalist Marc Andreessen splashed millions on beachfront homes
  • Inside Kris Jenner’s extravagant car collection, including Mercedes-Benzes, Bentleys and Rolls-Royces – but why did Ferrari ban the Kardashians from buying its limited-edition cars?
  • Who is British billionaire Alan Howard? Lady Gaga performed at his wedding, a US$12 million ceremony on Lake Como – he also splurges on NFTs, crypto and Monet artworks
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
